questions.text: According to the master sequence, which slide should immediately follow the Problem slide?
questions.explanation: The investor wants to see the fix right after understanding the pain. Delaying the solution slide loses attention.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

The investor wants to see the fix right after understanding the pain. Delaying the solution slide loses attention.

questions.text: What is the most common mistake founders make on their Team slide?

options.text: Showing only names without photos or LinkedIn links
Adding a CTO who can't code

questions.explanation: Investors want to verify credibility quickly. A name alone is meaningless; a LinkedIn profile and a photo build trust.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Investors want to verify credibility quickly. A name alone is meaningless; a LinkedIn profile and a photo build trust.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a weak "Ask" slide?

options.text: "We are raising $25,000 to launch mainnet and acquire 5,000 users."
"We are applying for the MST buildGrant ($25,000) to fund dev, audit, and marketing."

questions.explanation: A pitch without a concrete ask is incomplete. It leaves the investor with no call to action.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

A pitch without a concrete ask is incomplete. It leaves the investor with no call to action.

questions.text: Why should a Web3 pitch deck include a "Why MST?" callout?

options.text: Because the MST Grant Committee demands it
To demonstrate the dApp is natively built on MST, leveraging low gas, SARAL, or WASMify, which is a competitive advantage

questions.explanation: Showing that your dApp is better on MST because of its unique features signals strategic thinking and ecosystem alignment.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Showing that your dApp is better on MST because of its unique features signals strategic thinking and ecosystem alignment.

questions.text: What does the "Traction" slide primarily demonstrate to an investor?

options.text: That the product has a beautiful UI
That the team can execute and there is real user interest

questions.explanation: Traction proves that the team can ship and that the problem resonates with users. It is the strongest de-risking signal.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Traction proves that the team can ship and that the problem resonates with users. It is the strongest de-risking signal.

questions.text: "A pitch deck for a Web3 startup should include a deep technical explanation of the smart contract code on the Product slide."
"If your dApp does not yet have a token, you can skip the Tokenomics slide entirely."

Choose one of the 12 essential slides (other than Title and Ask) and explain what makes it particularly important for a Web3 startup applying for an MST grant. Provide a concrete example relevant to a dApp like KaamFi.
questions.explanation: 1 mark: identifies a specific slide (e.g., Tokenomics). 1 mark: explains its importance in a Web3/MST context (aligns incentives, shows sustainability). 1 mark: gives a concrete, realistic example tied to the chosen dApp.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Guide

1 mark: identifies a specific slide (e.g., Tokenomics).
1 mark: explains its importance in a Web3/MST context (aligns incentives, shows sustainability).
1 mark: gives a concrete, realistic example tied to the chosen dApp.

questions.text: Below is a draft of a Traction slide for a fictional dApp. Critique it in 3-4 sentences, and then rewrite it as a strong Traction slide bullet list.
questions.explanation: 1 mark: identifies vagueness, lack of numbers. 1 mark: provides specific numbers (e.g., "520 testnet users, 1,200 transactions"). 1 mark: includes a tangible metric and a timeline.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Guide

1 mark: identifies vagueness, lack of numbers.
1 mark: provides specific numbers (e.g., "520 testnet users, 1,200 transactions").
1 mark: includes a tangible metric and a timeline.

questions.text: Create a 10-15 slide pitch deck for your capstone project (or a hypothetical MST dApp) following the master sequence taught in this sub-module. Submit as a PDF.
questions.explanation:
Structure & Flow (5 marks): Slides follow the recommended order; logical narrative.
                    Problem & Solution Clarity (5 marks): Problem is visceral; solution is obvious and demos well.
                    Market & Traction (4 marks): TAM/SAM/SOM with sources; traction data (even testnet) is present.
                    Tokenomics & Business Model (4 marks): Token utility is clear; revenue/economic model explained.
                    Team & Ask (4 marks): Team faces and credentials; explicit Ask with allocation and milestones.
                    MST Integration (3 marks): At least one slide explicitly shows why MST (SARAL, WASMify, gas). Design and visual appeal (bonus consideration).
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Rubric (25 Marks)

Structure & Flow (5 marks): Slides follow the recommended order; logical narrative.
Problem & Solution Clarity (5 marks): Problem is visceral; solution is obvious and demos well.
Market & Traction (4 marks): TAM/SAM/SOM with sources; traction data (even testnet) is present.
Tokenomics & Business Model (4 marks): Token utility is clear; revenue/economic model explained.
Team & Ask (4 marks): Team faces and credentials; explicit Ask with allocation and milestones.
MST Integration (3 marks): At least one slide explicitly shows why MST (SARAL, WASMify, gas). Design and visual appeal (bonus consideration).

Submission: Upload a single PDF file named Capstone_PitchDeck_YourName.pdf. Include a link to your live testnet demo if available.
